The activity log now shows everything BombVault does, the whole app got a deep code-and-UI audit, and the folder-set domain has a clearer name.

## Added

- **The Activity Log now shows everything.** Off-site replication, retention prunes, restore drills, tamper tests and the flash-ZIP export are recorded as first-class runs alongside backups, restores and updates — each with duration and outcome, filterable like everything else. What happens in BombVault is now visible in BombVault.
- **Weekly digest.** An optional once-a-week summary through your existing notification channels: how many runs succeeded or failed, how much was backed up, whether every off-site copy is current, and the week's failures — one message instead of a feed to check.
- **Update check visibility.** Containers with "update after backup" now show when the image was last checked and the outcome (up to date / updated / check failed), so "no news" and "never reached" are no longer the same thing.
- **Backup engine cache limit.** The persistent restic cache introduced in v6.7.0 now cleans up after itself and respects a configurable size limit (default 4 GB), evicting the least-recently-used repository cache first.

## Changed

- **"Files" is now "Folders" ("Dateien" → "Ordner").** The domain backs up folder sets, so it is now named that way everywhere, in all 26 languages. Nothing changes technically — schedules, backups and settings carry over.
- **Retention pruning is batched.** Scheduled multi-item runs now prune once per domain after the loop instead of once per item — the same batching that v6.7.0 brought to off-site replication, applied to retention. Nightly runs get faster again; the batched prune appears in Run History like a manual one.
- **One hover style everywhere.** The customize pencil, heatmap pills and source toggles now fill on hover exactly like the app's tabs.

## Fixed

- **The recovery kit now requires a login password.** The kit contains the master key and off-site credentials — everything needed to decrypt every backup, including the append-only off-site archives designed to survive a compromised server. It is no longer downloadable while no login password is set; set one under Settings → Security first.
- **"Back up all" for folder sets now batches its off-site replication** like the container batch and the scheduled runs do, instead of re-opening the off-site repo once per set.
- **No more scrollbars in the Protection status card (#98).** At narrow or half width the card now wraps each domain row into a clean stacked layout instead of showing a horizontal scrollbar under every row; at full width the aligned column grid is unchanged.
- **Sessions can now be revoked.** "Sign out everywhere" (Settings → Security) invalidates every issued login session; existing sessions survive the upgrade until you use it.
- **The metrics token is no longer echoed** by the settings API — it reports only whether one is stored, like every other secret.
- **Storage card rows no longer clip their sparkline** at half width, and the backup heatmap no longer duplicates a day when clocks fall back.
- **Missing translations filled in:** tamper-test results, the schedule toggles and two stray keys are now properly translated in all 26 languages (they showed English before).

## Security & build hardening

- restic and rclone downloads are now checksum-verified against the official upstream hashes during the image build; a mismatch fails the build.
- All GitHub Actions are pinned to commit SHAs and base images to digests; Renovate keeps the pins current.
